name: youtube-publisher
notes:
SETUP GUIDE

1. HOW TO IMPORT
   n8n Settings → Workflows → Import → paste this JSON

2. CREDENTIALS TO CONFIGURE
   - Google OAuth2 (YouTube Data API v3): n8n Credentials → New → Google OAuth2 API
     Scope required: https://www.googleapis.com/auth/youtube.upload
     Name it exactly: 'YouTube Content Publisher'
     Note: YouTube upload REQUIRES OAuth2 with user consent — service accounts cannot upload to public channels
   - Supabase API: n8n Credentials → New → Supabase API
     Enter your Supabase project URL and service role key (NOT anon key)
     Name it exactly: 'Supabase Content Engine'

3. GCP SETUP (one-time)
   a. GCP Console → APIs & Services → Enable 'YouTube Data API v3'
   b. GCP Console → APIs & Services → Credentials → Create OAuth2 Client ID
      Type: Web application
      Name: n8n YouTube Publisher
   c. Authorized redirect URIs → add your n8n OAuth callback URL
      Format: https://[your-n8n-domain]/rest/oauth2-credential/callback
   d. Copy Client ID and Client Secret → paste into n8n Google OAuth2 credential

4. QUOTA AWARENESS (CRITICAL)
   - Default YouTube Data API v3 quota: 10,000 units/day
   - videos.insert cost: 1,600 units per upload
   - Maximum uploads per day: 6 (10,000 / 1,600 = 6.25 → floor to 6)
   - This workflow uploads 2 videos per run (long-form + Short) = 3,200 units/run
   - Safe daily runs: 3 (3 x 3,200 = 9,600 units, leaves 400 buffer)
   - If quota increase needed: YouTube API → Quotas → Request Increase (requires audit review)

5. PRIVACY STATUS
   Both long-form video and Shorts are uploaded as 'private'
   Review and publish manually in YouTube Studio after AI-generated content review
   This is intentional — the system creates drafts, you approve and make public

6. HOW TO ACTIVATE
   After credential setup: Toggle the workflow to Active (top-right switch in n8n)
   This workflow triggers automatically when content_bank.status changes to 'approved'

nodes.name: Supabase Trigger (approved status)
nodes.notes: Watches content_bank table for INSERT and UPDATE events. The IF filter below restricts to status=approved rows only. Trigger fires on ALL changes — filter is applied next.

nodes.name: Filter: status = approved
nodes.notes: Only proceed when content_bank row status = 'approved'. Ignores pending_review, draft, rejected, and all other statuses. This prevents accidental early publishing.

nodes.name: Filter: has processed_video
nodes.notes: Only proceed if processed_video GCS path is present. If content was approved before video processing completed, this gate prevents a failed upload attempt. The false branch is a no-op (workflow stops).

nodes.name: Code: Prepare Long-form Metadata + Signed URL
nodes.notes: Prepares YouTube video metadata for the long-form upload. Sets privacyStatus=private so founder reviews before making public. Category 27 = Education (appropriate for automation/business content). Parses GCS path into bucket and object components for signed URL generation.

nodes.name: HTTP Request: Generate GCS Signed URL (Long-form)
nodes.notes: Fetches the mediaLink from GCS for the processed video. The mediaLink is a pre-authenticated download URL. Alternative: generate a signed URL via the GCS signBlob API if service account credentials are available. YouTube requires the video to be at a publicly accessible URL during upload initiation.

nodes.name: HTTP Request: YouTube Upload Long-form (Resumable)
nodes.notes: Initiates a resumable upload session with YouTube Data API v3. QUOTA COST: 1,600 units per call. This returns a Location header with the upload URL — the actual video bytes are then PUT to that URL. For large files (>5MB, typical for video), resumable upload is required by YouTube API guidelines. privacyStatus=private ensures founder reviews before publishing.

nodes.name: Supabase: Store Long-form Video ID

fieldValues.fieldValue: ={{ JSON.stringify({ ...$('Supabase Trigger (approved status)').item.json.visual_assets, youtube_upload_initiated: true, youtube_upload_timestamp: new Date().toISOString() }) }}

nodes.notes: Stores the YouTube upload initiation timestamp in content_bank.visual_assets. After the resumable upload URL is returned, the actual video ID is only available after YouTube processes the upload — which is asynchronous. The youtube_upload_initiated flag prevents duplicate uploads on re-trigger.

nodes.name: Filter: has clips for Shorts
nodes.notes: Only proceed with Shorts upload if clips array is non-empty. Shorts require a 9:16 clip under 60 seconds. If no clips are available (video pipeline not yet run), skip Shorts upload gracefully.

nodes.name: Code: Select Best Clip by strength_score
nodes.notes: Selects the clip with the highest strength_score from visual_assets.clips array. The strength_score is set by the video clipper pipeline (10-02). Builds the Shorts metadata: title under 100 chars with #Shorts suffix, description includes #Shorts tag which is required for YouTube auto-classification as a Short (along with 9:16 aspect ratio + under 60s duration).

nodes.name: HTTP Request: YouTube Upload Short (Resumable)
nodes.notes: Initiates resumable upload session for the YouTube Short. Uses the SAME videos.insert endpoint as long-form — YouTube auto-classifies as Short based on: (1) 9:16 aspect ratio, (2) under 60 seconds duration, (3) #Shorts in description. QUOTA COST: 1,600 units. privacyStatus=private for founder review before publishing.

nodes.name: Supabase: Store Short Upload Initiated

fieldValues.fieldValue: ={{ JSON.stringify({ ...$('Supabase Trigger (approved status)').item.json.visual_assets, youtube_upload_initiated: true, youtube_short_upload_initiated: true, youtube_short_upload_timestamp: new Date().toISOString() }) }}

nodes.notes: Stores Short upload initiation timestamp in content_bank.visual_assets. The youtube_short_upload_initiated flag prevents duplicate Short uploads. Both flags together indicate this content item has had its full YouTube publishing initiated.

main.node: Code: Prepare Long-form Metadata + Signed URL
HTTP Request: Generate GCS Signed URL (Long-form)
Code: Select Best Clip by strength_score
